name: United States v. Hemani
short_description: A Second Amendment challenge to the federal ban on firearm possession by unlawful users of controlled substances.
legal_question: Whether 18 U.S.C. § 922(g)(3), which prohibits firearm possession by a person who is an unlawful user of or addicted to a controlled substance, violates the Second Amendment as applied to the respondent.
petitioner_argument_summary: The United States argues § 922(g)(3) is consistent with the Nation’s historical tradition of firearm regulation and that disarming unlawful drug users is permissible under the Second Amendment framework.
respondent_argument_summary: Respondent argues § 922(g)(3) lacks a sufficiently analogous historical tradition and that the statute is unconstitutional as applied.
